evaluatorFeedback.issue: sc-1-2 requires EVERY registered top-level command to have a '### bober <cmd>' heading in COMMANDS.md. The 5 named sections (config/telemetry/worktree/memory/facts) are done accurately. But `blackboard` — a registered top-level command (src/cli/commands/blackboard.ts, registerBlackboardCommand at src/cli/index.ts:348) — has NO proper level-3 heading; it only has a prose section '### Inter-child blackboard (Phase B)' (COMMANDS.md:500) with level-4 '#### agent-bober blackboard publish/read' subheadings. This is the ONLY remaining gap (all 34 other top-level commands have proper headings).
evaluatorFeedback.fix: Add a proper '### `bober blackboard`' level-3 heading in the SAME standard format as the other command sections (synopsis + subcommands + options), documenting the REAL registration: command `blackboard` desc 'Inter-agent fleet blackboard (publish/read findings)'; sub `publish <value>` desc 'Publish a finding to the shared fleet blackboard' option `--round <n>` (default 1); sub `read` desc 'Read findings from the shared fleet blackboard' option `--all` (default: siblings only). You MAY keep the existing 'Inter-child blackboard (Phase B)' conceptual explanation, but ensure a standard '### `bober blackboard`' command-reference heading exists so sc-1-2 holds. Keep the 5 sprint-1 sections intact. Re-verify: every top-level command from src/cli/index.ts register*Command calls + src/fleet/index.ts now has a matching '### `bober <cmd>`' heading.
evaluatorFeedback.note: This one additional edit is explicitly authorized by the orchestrator to resolve the sc-1-2 vs nonGoals#4 conflict — it is within the sprint's 'complete the reference' intent and the contract's evaluatorNotes named blackboard.
